pattern: Because a single move can already remove everything except either current end, the first player can always force the survivor to be the better of the array's two endpoints on turn one.
hints: A move can remove any subarray shorter than the current array - in particular, one move can remove everything except the current first element, or everything except the current last element.
Since that 'jump straight to one endpoint' move is always available on your turn, what is the most any player could ever hope to achieve versus what they can already guarantee immediately?
Alice moves first and can immediately end the game by keeping whichever of nums[0] or nums[-1] is larger - no interior element and no amount of extra play ever beats that.

edge_inputs.description: two elements, the first is larger
two elements, the second is larger
the interior holds the true maximum, but it cannot survive
